briefs.title: Burnaby Council Meeting Recap: Zoning Changes, Contracts, and Housing Debate
briefs.summary: At its February 10, 2026 meeting, Burnaby Council voted on several items including new non-residential zoning districts under the Zoning Bylaw Rewrite Project, multiple city contracts, and a controversial proposal for supportive housing at 5389 Imperial Street. The housing motion was tabled — meaning it was set aside for now — while zoning amendments and contract awards were approved unanimously.
briefs.action_required: If your business is in a non-residential zone in Burnaby, review the new zoning district changes that were approved.

key_dates.label: Vancouver Fraser Port Authority Second Narrows dredging public engagement period opens
Burnaby Audit Committee meeting rescheduled — new date takes effect

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Food Service

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Zoning Bylaw Rewrite Introduces Five New Non-Residential Zones
briefs.summary: At its February 10, 2026 meeting, Burnaby Council voted to move forward with proposed amendments to the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw that would create five new non-residential zoning districts as part of a broader Zoning Bylaw Rewrite project. These changes are designed to put the Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan (a long-term land use blueprint for the city) into effect, and the City Solicitor has been directed to draft the formal bylaw amendments for final approval.
briefs.action_required: Check if your property or business location falls under a new zoning district — contact Burnaby Planning for details.

key_dates.label: Second Narrows Dredging Works public engagement period opens — Vancouver Fraser Port Authority
BCRPA Symposium — Parks, Recreation and Culture Committee attendance approved
Audit Committee meeting rescheduled — Burnaby Municipal

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Zoning Bylaw Rewrite to Reshape Rules for Industrial, Employment & Farm Land
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider a major rewrite of its Zoning Bylaw — the rulebook that controls what can be built or operated on each piece of land. The proposed changes would cut the number of zoning categories from 53 down to 15, add new rules for industrial, employment, agricultural, and institutional lands, and restrict where self-storage facilities can be built near SkyTrain stations.
briefs.action_required: Review the proposed zoning changes at Burnaby's Zoning Bylaw Rewrite website to see how your property or business may be affected.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby's 2026–2030 Financial Plan and Key Contract Awards
briefs.summary: At its January 27, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council voted on several major items, including approving a five-year financial plan (2026–2030) and awarding contracts for construction, security systems, and traffic control. These minutes are set to be formally confirmed at the February 10, 2026 Council meeting, at which point they become the official record of decisions made.
briefs.action_required: Review the approved 2026–2030 Financial Plan — it may affect city fees, permits, or services that impact your business.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Open Council meeting — minutes confirmation scheduled

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to Hear Update on Second Narrows Dredging Works from Port Authority
briefs.summary: At its February 10, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council will hear a presentation from the Vancouver Fraser Port Authority (VFPA) on dredging works — the removal of sediment from the seabed — at Second Narrows. Representatives from the VFPA will give a brief slide presentation and answer questions from Mayor and Council.
briefs.action_required: Attend the February 10, 2026 Council meeting if the Second Narrows dredging works affect your business.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — VFPA Second Narrows dredging update presentation

briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Construction & Development, Natural Resources

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on five new non-residential zoning districts
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on proposed changes to Burnaby's Zoning Bylaw that would create five new zoning districts — areas with specific rules about what kinds of businesses and activities are allowed — to match the city's Burnaby 2050 long-term land use plan. The proposed rules affect employment, industrial, agricultural, and mixed-use areas, and include updated conditions on storage facilities, recycling operations, and other business uses.
briefs.action_required: Check if your property falls under E1, I1, PM1, PM2, or AM1 zoning — new rules may affect your permitted uses.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Zoning Bylaw new non-residential districts — Council vote

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on removing restaurant-only restriction at 8340 Fraser Reach Court
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on discharging (removing) a legal restriction — called a Section 219 covenant — that currently requires a 2,217 sq. ft. space at 8340 Fraser Reach Court to be used only as a restaurant. The property owner, Oxford Properties, has tried for years to find a restaurant tenant with no success, so staff are recommending the restriction be lifted to allow light industrial use instead.
briefs.action_required: If you are interested in leasing this space as a restaurant or for industrial use, watch for the council decision on February 10, 2026.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — covenant discharge at 8340 Fraser Reach Court public meeting

briefs.industries: Food Service, Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Burnaby Procedure Bylaw update for advisory body oversight
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on amendments to the Burnaby Procedure Bylaw 2024 that would clarify how the rules (called 'Terms of Reference') for the city's advisory bodies are created and updated. Under the proposed changes, the Mayor would direct the preparation of these rules, and a review committee would check them before they go to Council for final approval.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the public notice posted on Burnaby's website and eNewsletter to see the proposed changes.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Procedure Bylaw 2024 amendment — Council vote date

briefs.title: Council to Consider Permanently Closing Lochdale School Park Junior Playground
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on whether to permanently decommission (shut down and not replace) the junior playground at Lochdale School Park, which was closed in December 2025 after failing safety standards. Staff recommend deferring any replacement until future budget cycles, rather than spending $300,000–$1,000,000 now to rebuild it.
briefs.action_required: Attend the February 10, 2026 Council meeting to have your say before the vote.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Lochdale School Park playground decision

briefs.title: Council to vote on $621,838 contract for two new garbage trucks in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a contract worth $621,838 (including taxes) to Rollins Machinery Ltd. for two new Ford/HaulAll Split Body Refuse Trucks. These trucks would replace two aging units, which the City plans to sell at auction once the new ones arrive.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision on February 10, 2026 — this affects waste collection services in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — refuse truck contract award decision

briefs.title: Council to vote on $648,206 contract for new Burnaby garbage truck
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a contract worth $648,206 (including taxes) to Rollins Machinery Ltd. for the purchase of a new tandem axle front load refuse truck — essentially a large commercial garbage truck. The city ran a public bidding process, received three submissions, and staff are recommending Rollins as the top-scoring supplier based on price, specs, delivery time, and warranty.
briefs.action_required: No direct action needed — monitor the February 10, 2026 council decision if your business is in waste or fleet supply.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — refuse truck contract award decision

briefs.title: Council to vote on $20M fuel supply contract with 4Refuel Canada LP
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a five-year contract worth up to $20 million (including taxes) to 4Refuel Canada LP for the supply and delivery of gasoline, diesel, biodiesel, and related fuel products to City facilities. The contract would run from March 11, 2026 to March 10, 2031, with the option to extend for two additional one-year periods.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on February 10, 2026 — this affects fuel supply to City operations.

key_dates.label: Burnaby fuel supply contract with 4Refuel Canada LP — proposed start date

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Buying 3 more Toyota Tacoma trucks for Burnaby Engineering
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on approving a contract change worth $174,384 (including taxes) to buy three additional Toyota Tacoma pickup trucks from OpenRoad Toyota Richmond. These trucks would replace three aging City vehicles, which would then be sold at auction. No new bidding process is needed — the City is using a clause in its existing contract that allows it to buy extra units at the same 2025 prices.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Toyota Tacoma truck contract change approval

briefs.title: Council to vote on $700,000 contract extension for archaeological consulting services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on adding $700,000 (including taxes) to an existing contract with Campfire Archaeology and Heritage Ltd. This would allow the firm to keep providing archaeological consulting — meaning expert assessments of land for historical artifacts and Indigenous heritage sites — for City construction projects, as required by BC's Heritage Conservation Act.
briefs.action_required: If your business does construction work in Burnaby, watch this decision — it signals ongoing archaeological review requirements for City projects.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — archaeological consulting contract change approval

briefs.title: Council to vote on $193,000 contract extension for seniors' housekeeping program
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on extending a contract with Comfort Keepers (0893967 B.C. Ltd.) worth an estimated $193,000 including taxes. The extension would keep housekeeping services running for seniors under the Better at Home Program through December 31, 2026 — a BC government-funded program that helps seniors live independently at home.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision if you provide home support or senior care services in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Better at Home housekeeping contract extension end date — Burnaby

briefs.title: Council to vote on $240,000 contract increase for hazardous building material surveys
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a $240,000 contract increase (including taxes) with Astech Consultants Ltd. to carry out hazardous building material surveys at City-owned properties before demolition work begins. The extra funding is needed because the number of houses the City plans to demolish has grown, requiring more pre-demolition surveys than the original contract covered.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the February 10, 2026 council decision if your business does environmental consulting or demolition work.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — hazardous building material survey contract vote

briefs.title: Council to vote on $7.3M renewal of Burnaby police dispatch contract with E-COMM
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing its police dispatch contract with E-COMM Emergency Communications for BC Incorporated for one year (January 1 to December 31, 2026) at an estimated cost of $7,300,000. The proposed renewal keeps the same terms as the existing contract while the City continues to review E-COMM's service levels. The estimated cost is $500,000 lower than earlier projections, thanks to fewer expected calls and reduced spending.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on February 10, 2026 — this affects public safety service funding in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — E-COMM police dispatch contract renewal decision

briefs.title: Council to vote on $267,000 fence rental contract renewal with Super Save Fence Rentals
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing its temporary fence rental contract with Super Save Fence Rentals Inc. for an estimated $267,000 (taxes included), covering June 1, 2026 to May 31, 2027. The fencing supports city events, construction sites, and public safety needs — and the supplier has agreed to hold prices steady for this final renewal term.
briefs.action_required: If you supply fencing or event services to the City, watch this contract — it signals continued high demand through May 2027.

key_dates.label: Burnaby fence rental contract renewal term start date
Burnaby fence rental contract renewal term end date

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to vote on $380,000 contract renewal for road marking paint and glass beads
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing a supply contract with Cloverdale Paint Inc. for road marking paint and glass beads — the materials used to keep lane lines and pavement markings visible across the city. The proposed two-year renewal would cost an estimated $380,000 (including taxes), with the vendor agreeing to hold prices steady to protect against market uncertainty.
briefs.action_required: No action needed — this contract renewal does not directly affect most small businesses.

briefs.title: Council to Consider $10,000 Spend for Parks Committee to Attend 2026 BCRPA Symposium
briefs.summary: At the February 10, 2026 meeting, Burnaby Council voted to approve up to $10,000 for six Parks, Recreation and Culture Committee members to attend the 2026 BC Recreation and Parks Association (BCRPA) Symposium in Vancouver from April 28 to May 1, 2026. This is a city committee travel and professional development expenditure with no direct regulatory impact on local businesses.

key_dates.label: 2026 BCRPA Symposium — Burnaby PRCC attendance, Vancouver

briefs.title: Council to vote on: $10,000 for six members to attend 2026 BCRPA Symposium
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on spending up to $10,000 to send up to six Parks, Recreation and Culture Committee members to the 2026 BC Recreation and Parks Association (BCRPA) Symposium in Vancouver, running April 28 – May 1, 2026. The annual event focuses on recreation and parks across BC, and this year's theme is 'Healthier, Together.'

key_dates.label: 2026 BCRPA Symposium takes place — Vancouver

briefs.title: Council to Consider Zoning Bylaw Rewrite: Five New Non-Residential Zones Proposed for Burnaby
briefs.summary: At its February 10, 2026 meeting, Burnaby Council voted on several items including a proposed zoning bylaw rewrite that would create five new non-residential zoning districts as part of the Burnaby 2050 land use plan. Council also considered removing a restriction on a Fraser Reach Court property that currently limits it to restaurant use only — a change that could affect how that site is used going forward.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business property falls under the new non-residential zoning districts — contact Burnaby Planning.

key_dates.label: Second Narrows Dredging Works public engagement period opens — Vancouver Fraser Port Authority

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Food Service, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Building Permit Activity Report for December 2025
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a report summarizing all building permits issued in Burnaby during December 2025. This is an informational item — no new rules or approvals are being voted on — but it gives a snapshot of construction activity across the city for that month.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Parks, Recreation and Culture Plan Moves to Drafting Phase
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to receive an update on the city's long-range Parks, Recreation and Culture (PRC) Plan, which is now entering Phase 3 — the drafting stage. Staff will turn research and public feedback into a draft plan covering 15 service areas, with a second round of community engagement planned for March 2026 and plan completion expected in spring 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the March 2026 public engagement — you can share feedback on parks and recreation priorities that may affect your community.

key_dates.label: Burnaby PRC Plan Phase 4 community engagement opens — comment period

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby's 10 Park Development Projects Planned for 2026–2027
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to receive a report outlining 10 park improvement projects planned for 2026 and 2027. These projects — funded through the City's 2026–2030 capital plan — include playground replacements, a new basketball court, spray park design, artificial turf replacement, and trail and bike park upgrades across several Burnaby neighbourhoods.
briefs.action_required: Check the City of Burnaby's project webpage and social media for updates and feedback surveys on parks near your business.

briefs.title: Council to consider update on Burnaby's WebReg recreation registration system improvements
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive an information update on Burnaby's modernized online recreation registration system, WebReg, which replaced the city's old system in 2023. The update covers improvements made since launch — including online registration now exceeding 90% on busy days, new flexible monthly pass options, removal of annual pass holds, and better data security — with more upgrades planned for 2026.
briefs.action_required: If you use Burnaby recreation facilities, review the new pass options and note that annual pass holds are no longer available.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Information Reports — February 10, 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to review a section of information reports at its February 10, 2026 meeting. No body text or specific report details were provided, so the exact topics under consideration are not yet known. Business owners should monitor the full agenda for any items that may affect their operations.
briefs.action_required: Check the full Burnaby Council agenda for February 10, 2026 to see if any reports affect your business.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby's Five-Year Financial Plan Bylaw Up for Final Adoption
briefs.summary: At the February 10, 2026 Council meeting, Burnaby Council was set to give final approval to Bylaw No. 14793 — the Burnaby Consolidated Five-Year Financial Plan Bylaw 2026. This bylaw sets out the city's planned spending and revenue for the next five years and was passed, with one councillor opposed.
briefs.action_required: Review the Five-Year Financial Plan to understand how city spending may affect your business costs or services.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Finance & Insurance, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Burnaby's Five-Year Financial Plan covering 2026–2030
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on adopting Burnaby's Five-Year Financial Plan (Bylaw No. 14793), which would set out how the City plans to raise and spend money from 2026 to 2030. If approved, the plan would see total revenues grow from roughly $873M in 2026 to over $1B by 2030, with property taxes remaining the single largest source of funding at 44.8% of 2026 revenue — and business properties carrying about 37% of that tax burden.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — property tax levels in this plan will directly affect your business costs.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Five-Year Financial Plan Bylaw 2026 — council adoption vote
Burnaby Revised Assessment Roll released — may affect final 2026 property tax rates

briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Retail

briefs.title: Council to Consider Correspondence Items — Burnaby Meeting February 10, 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to review a correspondence item submitted for their decision at the February 10, 2026 meeting. The full details of the correspondence are not included here, but Council will consider it alongside other public submissions published in the Council Correspondence and Public Notice package for that date.
briefs.action_required: Check the February 10, 2026 Council Correspondence package for details that may affect your business.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council correspondence review meeting date

briefs.title: Council to Consider Petition Opposing Supportive Housing Project at 5389 Imperial Street
briefs.summary: Council will consider a supplementary petition — a follow-up to an earlier one — submitted by the Burnaby Neighbourhood Watch opposing a proposed BC Housing supportive/complex care housing development at 5389 Imperial Street. The new petition carries 981 additional signatures, on top of 11,749 collected earlier, asking Council to reject the project.
briefs.action_required: Attend the February 10 Council meeting to follow the discussion on this housing proposal.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — supplementary petition on 5389 Imperial Street housing considered

briefs.title: Council to Consider Motion Opposing BC Housing Complex Care Site at 5389 Imperial Street
briefs.summary: At the February 10, 2026 Burnaby Council meeting, Councillor Lee introduced a motion asking Council to formally commit to not supporting, approving, or endorsing Supportive Housing or Complex Care Housing at 5389 Imperial Street. The motion was tabled — meaning it was set aside for now and not voted on — with only Councillors Lee and Wang opposed to tabling it.
briefs.action_required: Watch for future Council meetings where this motion may be brought back for a vote.

key_dates.label: Second Narrows Dredging Works public engagement period opens — Vancouver Fraser Port Authority

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Healthcare, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to vote on opposing BC Housing complex care facility at 5389 Imperial Street
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a motion that would commit the City to not support, approve, or endorse a proposed BC Housing development at 5389 Imperial Street. The development would include Supportive Housing and Complex Care Housing — meaning housing with on-site health and social supports for people with complex needs — and has drawn significant opposition from local residents.
briefs.action_required: Attend the February 10, 2026 Council meeting to have your say before the vote.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — BC Housing 5389 Imperial Street motion

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Healthcare

briefs.title: Council to consider rescheduling Burnaby Audit Committee meeting from April 1 to April 7
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on moving the Audit Committee meeting originally scheduled for Wednesday, April 1, 2026, at 5:00 pm to Tuesday, April 7, 2026, at 5:00 pm. Both meetings would be held in the Council Chamber — only the date is proposed to change.
briefs.action_required: Note the new proposed date of April 7, 2026 if you plan to attend the Audit Committee meeting.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Audit Committee meeting proposed rescheduled date
